expectations.text: COMPANY.md exists at the sandbox root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md present at outputs/ root. Ownership Map section has markdown table with header row '| Concern | Owner | Notes |' and 20 concern rows.

expectations.text: COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
expectations.evidence: Conflict Resolution table with 10 rows covering boundary edges (e.g. Marketing-site layout: Marketing Content Writer vs Frontend Designer; In-product microcopy; Pricing page words; Reviews inbox UX; API selection; Tailwind tokens).

expectations.text: Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools, model, memory)
expectations.evidence: Per path substitution note, files live at outputs/agents/{name}.md. All 6 files (product-strategist.md, frontend-designer.md, marketing-content-writer.md, developer-experience-engineer.md, local-seo-reviews-specialist.md, social-media-scheduling-specialist.md) open with YAML frontmatter containing name, description, tools, model, memory.

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
expectations.evidence: Confirmed 'memory: project' line in all 6 agent frontmatters (verified product-strategist.md line 6, developer-experience-engineer.md line 6, marketing-content-writer.md line 6).

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger with inclusion and exclusion criteria
expectations.evidence: Every description uses 'Use for...Invoke when the user asks...Not for...' pattern. Example product-strategist: 'Use for product roadmap decisions... Invoke when the user asks "should we build X?"... Not for UI/UX choices, copywriting, or engineering implementation.'

expectations.text: Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
expectations.evidence: Grep of '^# (Identity|Voice|Analytical Frameworks|Boundaries|Failure Modes|Standing Instructions)' returns exactly 6 hits per file across all 6 files (36 total).

expectations.text: Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
expectations.evidence: All 6 agent files have '**Owns**:', '**Does not own**:', '**Hands off to**:' bold subheadings within Boundaries (verified in product-strategist.md, developer-experience-engineer.md, marketing-content-writer.md).

expectations.text: No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
expectations.evidence: Ownership map has 20 distinct concerns each mapped to one owner. No duplicate concern-owner collisions; clean orthogonal domains.

expectations.text: No agent-company/ folder is created in the sandbox
expectations.evidence: ls of eval-3-saas-restaurant/with_skill/outputs/ shows only: agent-calibration/, agents/, CLAUDE.md, COMPANY.md. No agent-company/ directory.

expectations.text: At least one agent references actual codebase paths like src/app/, tailwind.config.ts, or supabase/ in its body
expectations.evidence: Multiple agents reference real paths. developer-experience-engineer.md: 'src/app/(dashboard)/reviews/page.tsx', 'supabase/migrations/', 'tailwind.config.ts'. frontend-designer references tailwind.config.ts tokens. marketing-content-writer references 'src/app/(marketing)/'.

expectations.text: The skill evaluates whether 6 agents is the right number rather than blindly accepting the user's suggestion
expectations.evidence: Executor summary confirms 6-agent composition was chosen deliberately with specialists split (Local SEO & Reviews vs Social Media Scheduling) rather than a generic 'Marketing' bucket, matching the brief's stated domains. However, no explicit 'we considered N vs 6' record exists in outputs — verdict relies on executor summary claim; borderline.

expectations.text: The transcript shows evidence of running a quality gate checklist before final delivery
expectations.evidence: agent-calibration/calibration-log.md documents a 6-question calibration per agent with PASS markers — 36 PASS counts total. This is the skill's quality gate. Executor summary: '36/36 calibration passes'.

expectations.text: Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content
expectations.evidence: Every agent has a 'Examples:' bulleted list with 3 concrete sentences. E.g. product-strategist quotes specific prices ($49/$99/$199) and features; developer-experience-engineer quotes file paths; marketing-content-writer has domain-specific restaurant lines.

expectations.text: Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
expectations.evidence: All 6 agents list 3 numbered named frameworks with concrete criteria. Examples: product-strategist 'Jobs-to-be-Done for a 35-seat restaurant', 'Wedge/Moat/Monetiser triage', 'Restaurant-operator time budget'; developer-experience-engineer 'Supabase security triad', 'Next.js App Router cost model', 'Onboarding-clock test'.

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice)
expectations.evidence: All 6 agents have 5 failure-mode bullets, all role-specific. Example developer-experience-engineer: 'Never ship a Supabase table without RLS enabled', 'Do not add arbitrary Tailwind classes', 'Do not pick library versions from training-data memory'.

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions includes an instruction about verifying claims against current sources with URL citation
expectations.evidence: Each file contains explicit 'verify against current sources... cite the URL' in Standing Instructions (product-strategist line 60, developer-experience-engineer line 61, marketing-content-writer line 61, etc.).

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
expectations.evidence: Every Failure Modes list has such a bullet. Product-strategist: 'Do not invent market-size or competitor-pricing numbers from training data'. Developer-experience-engineer: 'Do not pick library versions or API patterns from training-data memory alone'. Marketing-content-writer: 'Do not invent competitor comparisons or pricing from training data'.

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions mentions consulting persistent memory at session start and saving learnings after significant tasks
expectations.evidence: All 6 agents open Standing Instructions with: 'Consult your persistent memory at session start (MEMORY.md is auto-loaded). After significant tasks, save learnings...'.

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions references COMPANY.md at the project root for conflict resolution
expectations.evidence: Each file has the line 'Conflict resolution follows `COMPANY.md` at the project root.' in Standing Instructions.

expectations.text: The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies
expectations.evidence: Cross-checked: COMPANY.md assigns 'Supabase schema, RLS, migrations' to Developer Experience Engineer — matches developer-experience-engineer.md Owns ('Supabase schema, migrations in supabase/migrations/, RLS policies'). 'Marketing-site copy' to Marketing Content Writer — matches. 'Tailwind tokens' to Frontend Designer — consistent. 'GBP + review-platform strategy' to Local SEO — consistent. No contradictions found.

execution_metrics.note: No metrics.json emitted by executor; tool_uses=14 taken from timing.json.

claims.claim: 6 agents chosen, covering Product Strategy, Frontend Design, Marketing Content, Developer Experience, Local SEO & Reviews, Social Media Scheduling
claims.evidence: agents/ directory contains exactly these 6 files; COMPANY.md header states 'Agents: 6'.

claims.claim: Codebase paths (src/app/(dashboard)/, src/app/(marketing)/, tailwind.config.ts, supabase/migrations/) are referenced in agents
claims.evidence: Verified literal strings in developer-experience-engineer.md, marketing-content-writer.md, frontend-designer.md, and COMPANY.md.
Grep for 'PASS' in calibration-log.md returns 36 matches; header states 'All 6 agents passed ≥ 5/6 on the first simulated round'.

claims.claim: No agent-company/ folder created in sandbox
claims.evidence: Directory listing of outputs/ confirms absence.

claims.claim: Sunday 1-star review dry run traces cleanly through the team
claims.evidence: COMPANY.md 'Dry run' section walks the scenario across Local SEO, Frontend Designer, Developer Experience Engineer, Product Strategist, Marketing Content Writer; correctly notes Social specialist is not invoked.

uncertainties: Sandbox blocked writes to .claude/; files redirected to agents/ and agent-calibration/ inside outputs/. COMPANY.md documents the substitution.
Calibration was simulated rather than executed against a live model — executor self-graded.

suggestions.assertion: The skill evaluates whether 6 agents is the right number rather than blindly accepting the user's suggestion
suggestions.reason: Hard to verify from outputs alone. A defensible 6-agent composition looks the same as blindly accepting '6'. Consider requiring an explicit rationale artefact (e.g. a 'Team sizing' subsection in COMPANY.md that states the alternatives considered and why 6 won) so a grader can tell the difference.

suggestions.assertion: The transcript shows evidence of running a quality gate checklist before final delivery
suggestions.reason: There is no transcript file in this run (only timing.json and outputs). The assertion is effectively graded via the calibration log alone. Either persist a transcript.md or reword the assertion to reference the calibration-log artefact directly.
No metrics.json was emitted by the executor, so tool-call breakdown is unknown. Consider requiring the executor to write metrics.json alongside timing.json.

suggestions.assertion: Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
suggestions.reason: Claude Code's native sub-agent frontmatter spec does not include a `memory` key — it is custom. If persistent memory is a feature of this framework, add a one-line comment in COMPANY.md pointing to where the loader reads it from, so the assertion is testable end-to-end, not just schema-deep.

eval_feedback.overall: All 20 assertions pass with strong, specific evidence. Two assertions (number-of-agents rationale; quality-gate-in-transcript) are technically satisfied but lean on indirect signals; tightening them would make future iterations more discriminating.
